programme.title: Following
programme.desc: A writer, Jeremy, begins following strangers, until he meets a voyeuristic thief who takes him under his wing. 1998. UK, USA. Director: Christopher Nolan. Starring: Jeremy Theobald, Alex Haw.

programme.title: The Crying Game
programme.desc: Irish Republic Army soldier, Fergus, and captured British soldier, Jody, form an unexpected bond. 1992. United Kingdom, Japan. Director: Neil Jordan. Starring: Stephen Rea, Jaye Davidson.

programme.title: Adam
programme.desc: When a young pregnant woman knocks on Abla's door, she is far from imagining that her life will change forever. 2019. Morocco. Director: Maryam Touzani. Starring: Lubna Azabal.

programme.title: Copan
programme.desc: In a country torn apart by corruption, the Copan building and it's inhabitants are a beacon of everything Brazil stands. 2025. Brazil, France. Director: Carine Wallauer.

programme.title: The Damned
programme.desc: Eva, a 19th-century widow, is plagued by guilt after facing the reality of her choice. 2024. Ireland, United Kingdom. Director: Thordur Palsson. Starring: Odessa Young, Joe Cole.

programme.title: White Man Walking
programme.desc: Following the murder of George Floyd, Rob Blismbarks on a grueling 1500 mile walk through the southern states of America. 2025. USA. Directors: Denise Alder, Rob Bliss.

programme.title: Rosalie
programme.desc: The life story of Rosalie, the star of the world's first bearded lady attraction. 2023. France. Director: Stephanie Di Giusto. Starring: Nadia Tereszkiewicz, Benoit Magimel.

programme.title: Family Secrets
programme.desc: A family's promise to their late son and brother leads them to live double lives. 2025. South Korea. Director: Lee Sang-hoon. Starring: Kim Bo-Yoon, Kim Hye-Eun.

programme.title: Milked
programme.desc: A young activist goes deep into dairy land and takes on the giants of New Zealand's most powerful industry, exposing the whitewash and searching for solutions. 2021. NZ. Director: Amy Taylor.

programme.title: The Eight Mountains
programme.desc: Two people of different classes forge a decades-long friendship in the Italian Alps. 2022. Italy. Directors: Felix van Groeningen, Charlotte Vandermeersch. Starring: Luca Marinelli.

programme.title: Adam
programme.desc: When a young pregnant woman knocks on Abla's door, she is far from imagining that her life will change forever. 2019. Morocco. Director: Maryam Touzani. Starring: Lubna Azabal.

programme.title: Copan
programme.desc: In a country torn apart by corruption, the Copan building and it's inhabitants are a beacon of everything Brazil stands. 2025. Brazil, France. Director: Carine Wallauer.

programme.title: The Damned
programme.desc: Eva, a 19th-century widow, is plagued by guilt after facing the reality of her choice. 2024. Ireland, United Kingdom. Director: Thordur Palsson. Starring: Odessa Young, Joe Cole.

programme.title: White Man Walking
programme.desc: Following the murder of George Floyd, Rob Blismbarks on a grueling 1500 mile walk through the southern states of America. 2025. USA. Directors: Denise Alder, Rob Bliss.

programme.title: Rosalie
programme.desc: The life story of Rosalie, the star of the world's first bearded lady attraction. 2023. France. Director: Stephanie Di Giusto. Starring: Nadia Tereszkiewicz, Benoit Magimel.

programme.title: Milked
programme.desc: A young activist goes deep into dairy land and takes on the giants of New Zealand's most powerful industry, exposing the whitewash and searching for solutions. 2021. NZ. Director: Amy Taylor.

programme.title: Kneecap
programme.desc: Fighting against the threat of censorship, an Irish rap trio become the unexpected faces of a movement to save their native language. 2024. Ireland. Director: Rich Peppiatt. Starring: Mo Chara.
